text: The darkness wraps around me like a cocoon, broken only by pockets of artificial light. Here, in this dim modern space, I feel a peculiar solitude—a mix of curiosity and mild unease. The sterile environment, so carefully designed for purpose, seems strangely impersonal in the absence of others. I am drawn to the hints of knowledge and research hinted at by the 'R+D' on the wall, yet I wonder what meaning persists in the stillness once all activity ceases. This emptiness prompts me to reflect on the roles spaces play, and how their essence shifts with or without human presence.
